summary: --password, --secret, and --auth-code flag help text now warns that passing the value on argv exposes it in shell history/process listings and points to --stdin as the safe path.
reason: Secret-as-CLI-flag sweep: these flags carry credentials/transfer auth codes through argv (visible in 'ps aux' and shell history). The flags are part of the generated contract and verify-skill gate so they cannot be removed; the secure --stdin path now is documented at point-of-use.
